  "findings": [
    "Conductors are undersized for the connected load and circuit length, causing excessive voltage drop.",
    "Installation does not meet NEC 310 ampacity requirements per Table 310.16.",
    "Undersized conductors pose a fire risk due to potential overheating."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately de-energize the circuit if energized.",
    "Remove non-compliant conductors.",
    "Recalculate conductor size considering load, length, voltage drop (recommend ≤3% for feeders, ≤5% total), and NEC Table 310.16 ampacity.",
    "Install correctly sized conductors and re-inspect before energizing."
  ],
  "rationale": "Installing conductors undersized for load and length is a clear violation of NEC 310 and standard trade practice. Voltage drop can lead to equipment malfunction and overheating, which is a safety hazard. The work must be corrected before proceeding.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 310.15 (Ampacity Tables)",
    "NEC Table 310.16",
    "NEC 210.19(A)(1) Informational Note 4 (Voltage Drop)",
    "NEC 215.2(A)(1) Informational Note 2 (Feeders Voltage Drop)"
  ]
}
